accessStepupMatrix.summary: Coarse IdP roles and scopes bootstrap sign-in posture, while elevated scopes drive source-backed step-up and approval gates without replacing Taxat's own authorization engine.
notes: Roles stay coarse and never imply client delegation or authority-of-record truth.
Step-up challenge timing comes from the Taxat corpus and is implemented through requested elevated scopes plus an Auth0 post-login Action posture.
Browser, native, machine, and limited-entry session law remain side by side in one pack.

source_refs.rationale: Taxat actor classes split client, tenant-human, service, and external boundaries before authorization is evaluated.
The IdP may bootstrap authentication posture, but the engine still owns the final ALLOW or REQUIRE_STEP_UP or REQUIRE_APPROVAL or DENY decision.
Browser, native, machine, and limited-entry session channels must remain distinct and challenge rotation must invalidate stale pre-step-up state.
Current Auth0 RBAC guidance allows coarse role and permission assignment while keeping richer authorization in the application.
Current Auth0 step-up guidance relies on requested scopes and Actions rather than a single tenant-wide MFA toggle.
Current Auth0 session guidance supports tenant defaults plus per-login overrides through Actions.

roles.label: Portal user

source_refs.rationale: Portal viewer and contributor are distinct actor classes, but both remain coarse client-portal entry postures at the IdP boundary.
Portal read and contribution capabilities are route-level affordances, not legal delegation truth.

notes: This role never proves delegation to a specific client or authority scope.

roles.label: Portal signatory

source_refs.rationale: CLIENT_SIGNATORY remains separate because sign-off may require STEP_UP_VERIFIED assurance.
Portal sign-off stays on the same approval-pack route and the IdP role cannot decide declaration legality on its own.

notes: This role is necessary for coarse signatory entry posture, but the engine still validates frozen approval-pack state and declaration basis.

roles.label: Operator workspace

source_refs.rationale: PREPARER and REVIEWER are tenant-side humans with shared operator-shell entry posture but different server-side legality later on.
The operator workspace spans browser and native shells while keeping the legal session model server-authoritative.

notes: This role does not automatically grant unmasked export, approval, or retention exception authority.

roles.label: Governance admin

source_refs.rationale: Governance admin posture covers the human actors that can legitimately request elevated authority-link, override, config, submission, or retention actions.
Governance commands that widen access or change retention posture are step-up or approval worthy and must remain explicit.

notes: A governance admin role is still only a coarse entry point. The engine decides the exact action tuple and bounded mutation legality.

roles.label: Audit review

source_refs.rationale: AUDITOR is a distinct tenant-side human actor class and should not inherit governance mutation scopes by default.
Unmasked provenance export is elevated even for auditors.

notes: Unmasked export remains elevated and request-scoped rather than baseline.

roles.label: Support operator

source_refs.rationale: SUPPORT_OPERATOR is a separate human operator class and should not be collapsed into governance admin.
Out-of-band annotations stay distinct from authority truth and therefore require their own bounded elevated scope.

notes: Support posture does not authorize silent truth rewrites or retention mutation.

roles.label: Service runtime

source_refs.rationale: Service principals remain first-class actors but never satisfy human step-up or approval paths.
Machine automation uses short-lived non-browser credentials and must not masquerade as an interactive session.

notes: Runtime service roles never imply human delegation or signatory legality.

roles.label: Provider bootstrap service

source_refs.rationale: The provider bootstrap boundary already exists as a distinct machine client family and needs its own coarse management scope.
IdP admin material must stay separated from general runtime application state.

notes: This role exists only for controlled provisioning and provider-management automation, not product-runtime access.

typed_gaps: The shared operating contract shared_operating_contract_0038_to_0045.md was absent, so the pack grounded itself directly in the named algorithm contracts, prior card outputs, and current Auth0 documentation.
notes: Roles stay intentionally coarse so the IdP never becomes the place where Taxat decides delegation, authority-link truth, or authority-of-record outcome.

source_refs.rationale: Taxat actor classes split client, tenant-human, service, and external boundaries before authorization is evaluated.
The IdP may bootstrap authentication posture, but the engine still owns the final ALLOW or REQUIRE_STEP_UP or REQUIRE_APPROVAL or DENY decision.
Browser, native, machine, and limited-entry session channels must remain distinct and challenge rotation must invalidate stale pre-step-up state.
Current Auth0 RBAC guidance allows coarse role and permission assignment while keeping richer authorization in the application.
Current Auth0 step-up guidance relies on requested scopes and Actions rather than a single tenant-wide MFA toggle.
Current Auth0 session guidance supports tenant defaults plus per-login overrides through Actions.

scopes.label: Portal read
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows the client portal shell to open after login; it does not prove delegation to any specific reporting subject.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still decides client scope, masking, and per-object legality.

source_refs.rationale: Portal viewer-style access is a coarse client-facing posture.

scopes.label: Portal contribute
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows onboarding and upload-capable entry posture once the normal authenticated portal session exists.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still validates upload-session binding, request scope, and stale-view guards.

source_refs.rationale: Upload and onboarding commands are typed backend commands and remain server-authorized.

scopes.label: Portal signatory base
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Marks that the account may enter signatory-capable portal surfaces before pack-specific legality is checked.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still decides if a particular approval pack is signable and whether fresh step-up proof is mandatory.

source_refs.rationale: CLIENT_SIGNATORY is distinct from viewer or contributor and may later need STEP_UP_VERIFIED assurance.

scopes.label: Operator workspace
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows the internal operator shell to open after sign-in.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: Per-client delegation, authority-link safety, and command legality remain server-authored.

source_refs.rationale: Operator and governance surfaces are shared shell vocabulary, but action legality is not string-matched from role names.

scopes.label: Governance read
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows access to governance-density routes and policy explainability surfaces.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: Mutation authority still depends on structured AUTHORIZE(...) results, not this coarse scope alone.

source_refs.rationale: Governance views must show separate permission, delegation, authority-link, and authn dimensions.

scopes.label: Audit read masked
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows masked audit and provenance inspection as a baseline read posture.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: Unmasked export still requires an elevated request plus server-side legality.

source_refs.rationale: ALLOW_MASKED is a first-class decision that must stay explicit.

scopes.label: Support workspace
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows support-oriented investigation surfaces without implying governance admin posture.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still decides if the support operator may annotate or export unmasked evidence.

source_refs.rationale: Support and audit investigation are explicit governed surfaces with their own viewer capability profiles.

scopes.label: Machine runtime
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows service-to-service machine identity for product runtime clients only.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still checks per-command PrincipalContext, idempotency, and service scope.

source_refs.rationale: Service principals stay first-class actors but never substitute for humans.

scopes.label: IdP bootstrap manage
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows the dedicated provider bootstrap client to manage Auth0-compatible control-plane settings.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: This scope is outside product-runtime authorization and remains limited to provisioning automation.

source_refs.rationale: The earlier IdP topology card established a separate provider-management bootstrap client family.

scopes.label: Elevated authority link
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requesting this scope is the coarse signal that an authority-link or relink operation needs fresh MFA-capable step-up.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still validates AuthorityBinding freshness, subject match, and bounded client scope.

source_refs.rationale: Linking or re-linking software to the authority is explicitly elevated.
Current Auth0 step-up guidance uses requested scopes plus Actions to trigger MFA for sensitive resources.

scopes.label: Elevated override approval
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before override approval affecting filing or parity outcome.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still validates rationale, expiry, exact scope, and stale-view basis.

source_refs.rationale: Override approval is explicitly elevated and bounded.

scopes.label: Elevated submit
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before operator-side submission or amendment send.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still validates approval state, trust, authority link, packet readiness, and send-time identity.

source_refs.rationale: Submission requires a current preflight sequence, not just a coarse elevated scope.

scopes.label: Elevated out-of-band annotation
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh human-gated posture before an out-of-band truth annotation is staged.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still keeps UNKNOWN and OUT_OF_BAND truth distinct from confirmed authority acknowledgement.

source_refs.rationale: Out-of-band truth cannot silently become confirmed authority truth.

scopes.label: Elevated unmasked export
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before full evidence or unmasked provenance export.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still evaluates masking rules, export posture, retention gates, and admissibility.

source_refs.rationale: Unmasked export is explicitly elevated.

scopes.label: Elevated config approval
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before compliance-mode config approval.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still verifies dependency topology hash, simulation basis hash, and bounded_safe_mutation posture.

source_refs.rationale: Governance mutation-capable actions preserve extra hashes and approval requirements beyond IdP posture.

scopes.label: Elevated retention exception
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before erasure, legal-hold release, or retention exception execution.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still checks legal-hold, retention minimum, and irreversible mutation guards.

source_refs.rationale: Retention exceptions are explicitly elevated and bounded.

scopes.label: Elevated governance mutation
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture for governance mutations that widen access, change step-up posture, or relink authority scope.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still binds the exact mutation tuple, affected client scope, and stale-view basis.

source_refs.rationale: Governance mutations that widen access or relink authority scope are elevated by source law.

scopes.label: Elevated client sign-off
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests a fresh MFA-capable signatory checkpoint when the current approval pack demands step-up.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still checks approval-pack hash, stale-view guard, declaration acknowledgement, and signability.

source_refs.rationale: Sign-off requires fresh step-up proof whenever the pack or frozen policy demands it.
Current Auth0 step-up guidance supports requesting elevated scopes to trigger MFA for sensitive routes.

notes: Elevated scopes are request-time challenge hints only; the backend still owns the final command-level authorization result.

source_refs.rationale: Taxat actor classes split client, tenant-human, service, and external boundaries before authorization is evaluated.
The IdP may bootstrap authentication posture, but the engine still owns the final ALLOW or REQUIRE_STEP_UP or REQUIRE_APPROVAL or DENY decision.
Browser, native, machine, and limited-entry session channels must remain distinct and challenge rotation must invalidate stale pre-step-up state.
Current Auth0 RBAC guidance allows coarse role and permission assignment while keeping richer authorization in the application.
Current Auth0 step-up guidance relies on requested scopes and Actions rather than a single tenant-wide MFA toggle.
Current Auth0 session guidance supports tenant defaults plus per-login overrides through Actions.

invalidation_events.label: Step-up completed
rotation_or_purge: Require revalidation of any pre-step-up command, cursor, or resumability artifact before reuse.
invalidation_events.effects_on_command_acceptance: Pre-step-up commands cannot be replayed blindly after the challenge state rotates.
invalidation_events.effects_on_resume_and_upload: Resume tokens, cursors, and upload-session control artifacts stay usable only if revalidated against the new challenge lineage.
invalidation_events.continuity_rule: Continuation returns to the same governed object and shell where the surface contract requires it.

invalidation_events.label: Session revoked or administrator invalidation
rotation_or_purge: Block future command acceptance until re-authentication.
invalidation_events.effects_on_command_acceptance: All future commands fail closed until a lawful replacement session exists.
invalidation_events.effects_on_resume_and_upload: Outstanding resume, scene-restoration, and upload control artifacts become revoked state rather than silently continuing.
invalidation_events.continuity_rule: Clients surface revoked-session recovery rather than inventing a soft refresh.

invalidation_events.label: Logout
rotation_or_purge: Treat logout as explicit session revocation.
Audit the revocation and clear interactive session material.
invalidation_events.effects_on_command_acceptance: Future interactive writes require a new authenticated session.
invalidation_events.effects_on_resume_and_upload: Browser or native resumability remains invalid until the next session re-establishes lawful scope.
invalidation_events.continuity_rule: No cached route context may silently regain live mutation posture after logout.

invalidation_events.label: Tenant or account switch
rotation_or_purge: Purge incompatible local caches and resume metadata.
Force re-resolution of principal class, client scope, delegation basis, and masking posture.
invalidation_events.effects_on_command_acceptance: Commands must not reuse stale bindings from the prior tenant or account.
invalidation_events.effects_on_resume_and_upload: Resume tokens, native scenes, and upload sessions from the old tenant become invalid or rebind-required.
invalidation_events.continuity_rule: Clients reopen only the narrowest still-lawful target after the new tenant context is established.

invalidation_events.label: Authority rebinding or binding drift
rotation_or_purge: Require new binding resolution and, where materially different, a new request identity.
Block delayed or queued send if token/client, subject, authority link, or approval or step-up evidence no longer match.
invalidation_events.effects_on_command_acceptance: Authority-integrated commands cannot continue on ambiguous or stale binding lineage.
invalidation_events.effects_on_resume_and_upload: Authority-adjacent continuations preserve pending-return posture but remain blocked until a fresh binding clears.
invalidation_events.continuity_rule: Return the user to the same object with explicit rebind or escalation posture instead of silently swapping authority context.

invalidation_events.label: Stale-view rejection or rebase
rotation_or_purge: Reject the command with typed stale-view metadata.
Preserve review progress only as read-only carry-forward where the surface contract says so.
invalidation_events.effects_on_command_acceptance: Sign-off, override, and other exact-basis actions require refresh onto the latest snapshot or pack before resubmission.
invalidation_events.effects_on_resume_and_upload: Latest resume token, snapshot, or replacement ref may be issued, but the client must not synthesize a merge.
invalidation_events.continuity_rule: Same route family and same object stay mounted where possible; unsafe mutation demotes into explicit review or recovery posture.

invalidation_events.label: Deep-link, invite, or limited-context entry expiry
rotation_or_purge: Require authenticated upgrade or re-authentication before sensitive mutation resumes.
Use typed fallback target and return-path metadata instead of guessing the next route.
invalidation_events.effects_on_command_acceptance: Upload, acknowledgement, and sign-off remain blocked until a fresh authenticated session and current object context exist.
invalidation_events.effects_on_resume_and_upload: Expired contextual entry never silently revives a stale upload or approval posture.
invalidation_events.continuity_rule: The user returns to the closest surviving same-object target or explicit fallback target with a reason code.

trigger_rows.label: Linking or re-linking software to the authority
trigger_rows.assurance_requirement: Fresh human gate evidence; no machine substitution.
revalidation_requirements: Resolve a fresh AuthorityBinding if subject, client, authority scope, or link lineage changed.
Do not continue from stale deep-link or ambient route state alone.

trigger_rows.label: Approve override changing filing readiness or parity outcome
trigger_rows.assurance_requirement: Fresh approver identity with current view and policy basis.
revalidation_requirements: Refresh on stale-view rejection before resubmitting.

trigger_rows.label: Submit filing or amendment

revalidation_requirements: Verify current approval or step-up state before envelope build.

trigger_rows.label: Mark externally unverified submission as known out-of-band

revalidation_requirements: Keep out-of-band truth distinct from confirmed authority truth.

trigger_rows.label: Export full evidence packs or unmasked provenance

revalidation_requirements: Re-evaluate masking scope and export posture before materializing bytes.

trigger_rows.label: Execute erasure, legal-hold release, or retention exception

trigger_rows.label: Client signatory declaration or sign-off when the approval pack demands step-up

trigger_rows.label: Governance mutation that widens access, changes step-up posture, alters retention minimums, or relinks authority scope

session_profiles.label: Browser operator session
session_profiles.session_carrier: Secure HttpOnly same-site session cookie plus anti-CSRF binding.
session_profiles.entry_posture: OIDC/OAuth human sign-in through the external IdP, then server-mediated interactive session resolution.
session_profiles.storage_boundary: Backend session store plus browser-safe derivable cache only.
notes: Operator browser posture defaults to non-persistent cookies and no browser-facing refresh tokens.

session_profiles.label: Browser portal session
session_profiles.session_carrier: Secure browser session cookie plus anti-CSRF binding after authenticated upgrade.
session_profiles.entry_posture: Normal sign-in or post-invite upgrade into a fully authenticated portal session bound to one principal class and masking posture.
session_profiles.storage_boundary: Server-authored session plus client-safe resumability only.
notes: Portal sessions stay persistent but bounded; long-lived browser refresh tokens are intentionally not enabled.

session_profiles.label: Native macOS operator session
session_profiles.session_carrier: ASWebAuthenticationSession or equivalent plus Keychain-backed product-session artifacts.
session_profiles.entry_posture: System-browser or platform auth-session sign-in only; no embedded webview primary login.
session_profiles.storage_boundary: Keychain and OS-protected tenant-bound cache only.
notes: Native public clients use rotating refresh tokens with expiring lifetime and reuse detection.

session_profiles.label: Machine automation client
session_profiles.session_carrier: Short-lived machine credential plus explicit command_id and idempotency_key.
session_profiles.entry_posture: Short-lived non-browser client credential with explicit service identity and scoped environment binding.
session_profiles.storage_boundary: Managed runtime secret boundary or vault-backed credential retrieval only.
notes: Machine actors never satisfy REQUIRE_STEP_UP or REQUIRE_APPROVAL for humans.

session_profiles.label: Invite or deep-link pre-upgrade
session_profiles.session_carrier: Contextual route and return-path state only.
session_profiles.entry_posture: Notification, email invite, or deep link opens focused route context before the normal authenticated session has been re-established.
session_profiles.storage_boundary: Local route context only; no trust-sensitive mutation authority.
notes: This is not a full IdP-authenticated mutation session and cannot satisfy sign-off or upload on its own.

session_profiles.label: Governed upload-session transfer
session_profiles.session_carrier: Normal authenticated session plus governed upload-session binding contract.
session_profiles.entry_posture: Authenticated portal session allocates a governed ClientUploadSession as the byte-transfer exception to the generic command surface.
session_profiles.storage_boundary: Derivable transfer metadata only; attachment finalization remains a typed command.
notes: Upload transfer is not a substitute for signatory or authority-sensitive step-up.
Browser, native, machine, limited-entry, and governed upload-session posture remain side-by-side so later agents do not re-derive session law from scattered prose.

source_refs.rationale: The vendor overlay must remain secondary to first-party audit evidence and operational telemetry.
All vendor-visible monitoring still depends on the corpus correlation keys that bind events back to manifest and authority lineage.
Trace and error overlays need release and manifest-rooted runtime lineage rather than free-floating exception feeds.
Alert posture must focus on actionable reliability, security, and release regressions instead of vanity noise.
Monitoring capture must redact secrets, tokens, and high-risk payloads before they ever become vendor-visible.
Sampled or filtered telemetry may never become the proof-of-record substitute for audit evidence.
Privacy minimization outranks diagnostic convenience, so the monitoring overlay must fail closed when settings drift.
Visibility and signal separation remain explicit even when several telemetry lanes share identifiers.
Diagnostic logging must not reintroduce personal or authority-secret data that minimization policy already excludes.
Vendor DSNs and automation tokens must stay inside the governed secret boundary and only safe refs may persist in repo artifacts.
Release and operational security gates require explicit health and secret-rotation posture before promotion.

outcome_rows.label: 1. Outcome
outcome_rows.detail: If route, session, or review state drifted, reopen from a known entry route instead of replaying old actions.

evidence_rows.label: 1. Evidence
evidence_rows.detail: Security-review copy retained as redacted safe snapshot and route fingerprint only.

redaction_rows.label: 1. PII posture
redaction_rows.detail: IPs and mailbox hints are redacted in safe-copy evidence.

redaction_rows.label: 2. Trace posture
redaction_rows.detail: Suppressed because session material may be visible during review.

resume_precondition_rows.label: 1. Precondition
resume_precondition_rows.detail: Expected successor route or safe dashboard home is visible.

resume_precondition_rows.label: 2. Precondition
resume_precondition_rows.detail: Session rotation or forced re-auth has been revalidated.

source_refs.rationale: The shared operating contract requires manual checkpoints to persist durable artifacts, sanitized evidence, and explicit resume rules.
Step-up, CAPTCHA, and human-confirmation actions must fail closed and may not be bypassed by a machine actor.
Resume artifacts must be revalidated against route identity, session validity, and post-step-up continuity before mutation resumes.
Manual checkpoint evidence must stay sanitized and must not persist one-time codes or other sensitive materials.
Checkpoint atlases and resume diagnostics must remain automation-verifiable, keyboard reachable, and stable under reduced motion.
Checkpoint artifacts require durable audit-safe structure instead of being buried in ephemeral telemetry noise.

scenarios.label: Provider policy or rate-limit block
scenarios.summary: Policy blocks and rate limits are not resumable by guesswork. They require human review or provider posture change before a run may reopen from a known entry route.

automation_path_rows.label: 1. Recipe step
automation_path_rows.detail: The portal rejected the current attempt before the intended admin workflow could continue.

automation_path_rows.label: 2. Last safe step
automation_path_rows.detail: The run recorded the block without retry storms or hidden backoff loops.

checkpoint_rows.label: 1. Checkpoint reason
checkpoint_rows.detail: Provider policy, throttling, or blocked posture surfaced instead of a human-verifiable challenge.

checkpoint_rows.label: 2. Pause posture
checkpoint_rows.detail: The checkpoint remains open for review, not for blind continuation.

human_step_rows.label: 1. Human action
human_step_rows.detail: Review provider posture, rate limit, or admin policy before any retry.

human_step_rows.label: 2. Human role
human_step_rows.detail: Security or platform operator with authority to change the blocking condition.

resume_rows.label: 1. Resume verification
resume_rows.detail: Reopen from a known entry route after the block is cleared.

resume_rows.label: 2. Fail-closed
resume_rows.detail: Any unknown successor route or stale session forces a new checkpoint or review.

outcome_rows.label: 1. Outcome
outcome_rows.detail: Continue only after a fresh verification read proves the block is cleared.

evidence_rows.label: 1. Evidence
evidence_rows.detail: Route fingerprint, redacted copy snapshot, and policy-block reason are retained.

redaction_rows.label: 1. Retry posture
redaction_rows.detail: No trace archive or repeated screenshots are kept for blocked-policy loops.

resume_precondition_rows.label: 2. Precondition
resume_precondition_rows.detail: Route reopened from a known entry point.

scenarios.label: Unknown challenge review
scenarios.summary: Unknown portal challenges fail closed into review so provider drift and blocked security posture never collapse into a hopeful continue.

automation_path_rows.label: 1. Recipe step
automation_path_rows.detail: The portal rendered a challenge that did not match known reason families.

automation_path_rows.label: 2. Last safe step
automation_path_rows.detail: Automation captured enough sanitized evidence to support review, then stopped.

checkpoint_rows.label: 1. Checkpoint reason
checkpoint_rows.detail: The challenge is unclassified and therefore review-only.

checkpoint_rows.label: 2. Pause posture
checkpoint_rows.detail: Unknown challenge remains distinct from selector drift and generic failures.

human_step_rows.label: 1. Human action
human_step_rows.detail: Review provider wording, route identity, and operator-safe next steps.

resume_rows.label: 1. Resume verification
resume_rows.detail: Reopen from a known route only after the challenge is classified or cleared.

outcome_rows.label: 1. Outcome
outcome_rows.detail: No continuation is allowed from an unknown challenge without explicit human review.

evidence_rows.label: 1. Evidence
evidence_rows.detail: Masked screenshot, DOM hash, safe-copy snapshot, and route fingerprint are preserved.

redaction_rows.label: 1. Redaction posture
redaction_rows.detail: Safe-copy evidence strips OTPs, mailboxes, and IPs before storage.

resume_precondition_rows.label: 1. Precondition
resume_precondition_rows.detail: Unknown challenge has been classified or cleared.

resume_precondition_rows.label: 2. Precondition
resume_precondition_rows.detail: Selector drift has been checked separately.

notes: The atlas is a pause-and-resume ledger, not a support inbox or NOC dashboard.
Reason families, evidence posture, and resume preconditions stay visible together so later provider adapters do not invent checkpoint law ad hoc.
Reduced motion swaps translation for opacity and focus-state emphasis without changing information order.

secretRootTopologyLedger.summary: One authoritative ledger for alias families, key hierarchy, environment partitions, and least-privilege grants. Provider selection remains blocked on the later platform choice, but names, namespaces, and permissions are now frozen.
notes: Sandbox, preprod, and production use the same alias families but never the same concrete namespace.
Authority credentials remain under a distinct wrapping family from general runtime secrets.
Break-glass access is explicit, production-scoped, and dual-control bound.

aliases.label: postgresql/control-store/password
notes: Runtime write paths may read this secret; projections and support tools may not.

aliases.label: postgresql/audit-store/password
notes: Audit retention law applies to metadata, not to plaintext password re-exposure.

aliases.label: cache/resume/auth-token
notes: Cache credentials stay separate from database credentials to bound blast radius.

aliases.label: token-sealing/active-kek-metadata
notes: Carries key identity and rotation lineage only; no raw data key material belongs in repo-tracked artifacts.

aliases.label: hmrc/client-secret/web-app-via-server
notes: Plaintext may be seen only during provider reveal and immediate ingestion.

aliases.label: hmrc/client-secret/desktop-app-via-server
notes: Native operator auth still terminates through the governed gateway boundary.

aliases.label: hmrc/client-secret/application-restricted
notes: Application-restricted credentials remain separate from user-restricted app registrations.

aliases.label: idp/browser-client-secret
notes: Browser client secrets are runtime-held only for confidential web clients; public native clients remain metadata-only.

aliases.label: idp/machine-client-secret
notes: Machine client secrets must never be copied into projection or support surfaces.

aliases.label: email/server-token
notes: Local provisioning keeps only the bootstrap capture alias; shared runtime holds the active server token.

aliases.label: email/webhooks/header-secret
notes: Transport evidence may reference this alias, but delivery callbacks never become workflow truth.

aliases.label: monitoring/sentry/org-automation-token
notes: Automation tokens remain distinct from DSN references and never cross into audit truth.

aliases.label: monitoring/sentry/ingest-dsn
notes: DSN rotation may be rare, but the alias remains governed and environment-partitioned.

aliases.label: push/native-provider-key-bundle
notes: APNs and FCM material stays inside the same governed boundary but remains logically distinct from email and monitoring secrets.

aliases.label: support/adapter/shared-secret
notes: The repository currently freezes this as future-safe adapter posture, not a selected external helpdesk runtime.

aliases.label: evidence/document-extraction/provider-api-key
notes: Provider remains unselected; this alias family is frozen to prevent ad hoc names later.

aliases.label: uploads/malware-scanning/provider-api-key
notes: Provider remains unselected; this alias family is frozen to prevent namespace drift later.

aliases.label: bootstrap/operator-token
notes: Bootstrap credentials remain short-lived and never enter CI or shared runtime namespaces.

aliases.label: break-glass/recovery-material
notes: Break-glass remains dual-control, auditable, and absent from day-to-day runtime grants.

aliases.label: provisioning/redaction-dictionary
notes: This is governed metadata rather than a bearer secret, but it still belongs in the same auditable boundary.

keyNodes.label: Provider-managed default secret-store key
keyNodes.hardware_posture: Allowed only for local, CI, and temporary bootstrap scenarios when customer-managed roots are not yet adopted.

keyNodes.label: Customer-managed secret-store KEK
keyNodes.hardware_posture: Primary phase-01 root for sandbox, preprod, production, and drill partitions.
keyNodes.rotation_mode: Rotate under provider KMS cadence with append-only lineage and explicit cutover attestation.

keyNodes.label: Optional HSM-capable wrapping root
keyNodes.hardware_posture: Reserved for production-like, authority, and break-glass partitions where later compliance work may require hardware-backed wrapping.
keyNodes.rotation_mode: Enabled by provider/platform choice, not by default before that choice exists.

keyNodes.label: Runtime configuration wrapping key
keyNodes.hardware_posture: Wraps service secrets for runtime stores, monitoring tokens, and operational callbacks.

keyNodes.label: Authority credential wrapping key
keyNodes.hardware_posture: Exclusive to HMRC and similar authority credentials, isolated from ordinary runtime configuration.
keyNodes.rotation_mode: One-time capture followed by append-only secret-version lineage.

keyNodes.label: Provider one-time-ingest wrapping key
keyNodes.hardware_posture: Protects secrets that are revealed once by providers and then stored only as governed refs and versions.
keyNodes.rotation_mode: Capture once, attest immediately, suppress plaintext thereafter.

keyNodes.label: Bootstrap and tooling wrapping key
keyNodes.hardware_posture: Contains local/bootstrap-only material and governed redaction metadata.
keyNodes.rotation_mode: Short-lived tokens, metadata-only refs, and explicit purge after bootstrap windows.

keyNodes.label: Break-glass and restore-material wrapping key
keyNodes.hardware_posture: Reserved for dual-control recovery bundles and restore-material references.
keyNodes.rotation_mode: Annual drill and immediate post-use revoke posture.

accessRoles.label: Bootstrap operator
notes: Seeds or adopts the boundary, but must not rely on general-purpose read-back of stored secrets.

accessRoles.label: CI deploy role
notes: May inspect metadata and attest config identity only.

accessRoles.label: Runtime API
notes: May consume runtime config and callback verification secrets required for interactive request handling.

accessRoles.label: Runtime worker
notes: Handles authority exchange, provider callbacks, notifications, and upload pipeline tasks.

accessRoles.label: Projection service
notes: Projection builders do not need plaintext secrets and remain metadata-only.

accessRoles.label: Support adapter
notes: May consume only the future-safe adapter secret and metadata required for webhook validation.

accessRoles.label: Observability agent
notes: May consume monitoring-specific tokens or DSNs but not broader runtime or authority credentials.

accessRoles.label: Security rotation automation
notes: Can write, rotate, disable, and attest versions but should not serve as a general read client.

accessRoles.label: Audit reader
notes: Read-model and audit tooling remains metadata-only.

accessRoles.label: Break-glass operator
notes: Dual-control, production-bound, and explicitly auditable.
Bootstrap creates placeholders and policies but does not serve as a routine secret reader.
CI may prove deterministic config identity but must never receive raw or decryptable secrets.
Interactive runtime may consume only runtime-bound config and callback verification secrets.
Workers are the only routine non-break-glass path allowed to consume authority and provider secrets.
Projection flows are explicitly denied secret reads even where they can inspect non-sensitive metadata.
Support tooling is limited to the explicit adapter and callback boundary.
Observability remains fenced to observability-specific secrets.
Rotation automation acts through versioned write and revoke operations instead of standing read access.
Audit readers may inspect refs, lineage, and receipts but not secret values.
Break-glass is deliberately narrow, production-scoped, and dual-control bound.

controlAndAuditStoreLedger.topologyModeLabel: Single cluster / two databases / hard role boundary
controlAndAuditStoreLedger.summary: The transactional control store and append-only audit store are frozen as two separately credentialed PostgreSQL databases per environment. Provider product names, regions, and exact managed features remain unresolved, but the durable truth boundary, role model, and PITR gates are now explicit.
notes: Read replicas and restore drills are explicitly non-authoritative for commit truth.
Append-only evidence is protected by insert-only grants, update/delete rejection triggers, and stream-head monotonicity checks.
Contract phases remain blocked until reader-window, restore, and fail-forward rules all stay green.

stores.label: Control Store
stores.topology_posture: Transactional source of truth for manifests, workflow, receipts, upload state, authority state, retention controls, and migration metadata.
stores.order_guarantee: Row-level business ordering is domain-specific; cross-object legal truth remains in transactional control tables, not in the broker or projections.
stores.retention_or_restore_posture: Tier 0 direct backup required; restore evidence and reopen gates persist in restore_verification schema.
notes: Later phase tables land in the named control schemas instead of mixing mutable truth and restore metadata arbitrarily.

stores.label: Audit Store
stores.topology_posture: Append-only evidence ledger for audit events, ingress receipts, provenance, replay attestations, and erasure proof references.
stores.order_guarantee: Canonical event order is enforced per stream through stream heads plus indexes on audit_stream_ref and stream_sequence.
stores.retention_or_restore_posture: Tier 0 direct backup required; retention acts by detaching expired partitions rather than mutating rows in place.
notes: Mutable admin metadata is limited to stream heads and partition-maintenance support; evidence rows themselves stay append-only.

stores.label: Read Replica Profile
stores.topology_posture: Read replicas may feed projections, investigations, or restore verification reads, but they never become commit truth.
stores.order_guarantee: Replica lag is observable only and cannot replace control or audit primary ordering guarantees.
stores.retention_or_restore_posture: Replica freshness must remain below the allowed lag threshold before it can serve non-authoritative read workloads.
notes: The viewer and runbook make replica-lag non-authoritative explicitly to close the common truth-source confusion gap.

stores.label: Restore Drill Profile
stores.topology_posture: Restore drills hydrate a new control plus audit pair from backup plus WAL and stay blocked until all reopen gates pass.
stores.order_guarantee: Restore validation checks audit continuity, queue rebuild, and authority rebuild against the restored stream heads and control metadata.
stores.retention_or_restore_posture: Read-only until restore evidence, privacy reconciliation, audit continuity, queue rebuild, authority rebuild, and authority binding revalidation are all verified.
notes: Restore drills are promotion evidence only when bound to the same release-candidate identity and schema-reader window posture.

roles.label: Control owner
notes: Object ownership is separated from runtime use so application login roles do not own mutable truth tables.

roles.label: Control migrator
notes: Migration DDL stays explicit and phase-bound; contract phases are blocked until reader-window and restore posture allow them.

roles.label: Runtime API
notes: Runtime API may mutate control truth but cannot mutate audit evidence in place.

roles.label: Manifest orchestrator
notes: The orchestrator coordinates lifecycle and receipts but remains separated from DDL ownership.

roles.label: Stage worker
notes: Workers can advance durable state only through the specific control schemas they own operationally.

roles.label: Projector read-only consumer
notes: Read-side consumers rebuild projections from control truth but never write back guessed legal state.

roles.label: Audit owner
notes: Audit ownership remains separate from append writers and investigators so evidence mutation paths stay closed.

roles.label: Audit append writer
notes: Append writers can only insert new audit rows and update stream heads via trigger-owned logic.

roles.label: Audit investigator
notes: Investigators can read append-only evidence and stream heads but cannot alter rows or sequence state.

roles.label: Partition maintainer
notes: Retention acts at partition boundaries; row mutation remains forbidden even for maintenance operators.

roles.label: Backup and restore operator
notes: This role exists for drill and recovery work and still cannot bypass privacy, queue, or authority reopen gates.

roles.label: Break-glass operator
notes: Break-glass is read-mostly and approval-bound; it does not create a silent mutable bypass around the audit ledger.

restorePolicies.label: Restore gates
restorePolicies.wal_summary: WAL archive must be continuous and tested before base backups are treated as restorable.
restorePolicies.note: A checkpoint cannot claim VERIFIED or READY_FOR_REOPEN until every restore gate is explicitly satisfied.

restorePolicies.label: PITR and WAL
restorePolicies.wal_summary: wal_level=replica, archive_mode=on, archive_timeout=60s for authoritative environments.
restorePolicies.note: WAL deficiencies block reopen and must surface as restore-not-ready posture.

restorePolicies.label: Migration window
restorePolicies.wal_summary: Expand, backfill, and contract phases remain bound to reader-window and rollback safety.
restorePolicies.note: Destructive contract phases remain blocked until restore, replay, and rollback boundaries all stay lawful.

storageBucketTopologyBoard.isolationPostureLabel: Environment-scoped buckets / immutable refs / quarantine hard boundary
storageBucketTopologyBoard.summary: Taxat's object-storage substrate is fixed as environment-separated purpose buckets with immutable refs, versioned recovery posture, quarantine isolation, masked-vs-restricted export separation, and logical event routes that remain broker-agnostic until the queue layer is provisioned.
notes: Bucket listings and direct object URLs never define truth, currentness, or admissibility.
Quarantine release uses copy/promote semantics with history retained.
Lifecycle classes are fixed now even though exact day-count durations remain a source gap.

lifecycleRules.label: Upload staging

lifecycleRules.label: Retained evidence

lifecycleRules.label: Authority payload bodies

lifecycleRules.label: Restore archive

eventRoutes.label: Upload finalized -> scan request
eventRoutes.note: Queue or broker resolution stays logical until pc_0052, but dedupe and replay posture are already fixed here.

eventRoutes.label: Upload metadata change -> validation recheck
eventRoutes.note: Metadata changes must not silently widen preview or attachment posture.

eventRoutes.label: Retained evidence finalized -> manifest binding
eventRoutes.note: Evidence bodies become addressable via immutable refs only after durable metadata binding lands.

eventRoutes.label: Authority payload finalized -> ingress normalization
eventRoutes.note: Payload body storage and authority truth remain distinct.

eventRoutes.label: Derived preview finalized -> preview registration
eventRoutes.note: Preview derivatives are never lawful direct-download targets by themselves.

eventRoutes.label: Masked export finalized -> delivery attestation
eventRoutes.note: Masked exports and restricted exports are attested separately.

eventRoutes.label: Restricted export finalized -> operator delivery attestation
eventRoutes.note: Restricted export delivery remains distinct from customer-safe externalization.

eventRoutes.label: Quarantine object finalized -> quarantine audit
eventRoutes.note: Quarantine copy/promote workflows keep the original quarantined version immutable.

eventRoutes.label: Restore archive finalized -> restore index
eventRoutes.note: Restore archive indexing is evidence-supporting and remains non-authoritative for reopen by itself.

eventRoutes.label: Lifecycle or delete -> retention law audit
eventRoutes.note: This route is logical and cross-purpose even though it is anchored here for the topology board.

messageFabricAtlas.topologyModeLabel: Transactional outboxes + authenticated dedupe inboxes + rebuildable broker
messageFabricAtlas.summary: Durable outboxes, authenticated dedupe inboxes, and partitioned broker channels keep transport ephemeral and recoverable. Broker loss is delivery loss only; truth remains in control, audit, and immutable refs.
notes: The broker is not the system of record for manifests, authority state, workflow, or retention control.
Authority/provider callbacks enter the authenticated dedupe inbox before any legal-state mutation.
Payloads stay opaque and secret-free in transit. Durable tables carry the real lineage and admissibility basis.

families.label: Stage Orchestrator / Workers
families.center_lane_emphasis: Manifest stage dispatch and worker claims
families.description: Durable stage receipts publish transport work without allowing the broker to become manifest truth.
families.note: A broker gap may slow dispatch, but it must not create a second legal source of stage ownership or status.

families.label: Upload / Evidence Processing
families.description: Object-change routes become durable work only after immutable refs, request lineage, and scan posture are frozen.
families.note: Object store notifications are already logical channels from pc_0051; this family binds them to one broker fabric without changing their truth boundary.

families.label: Authority Send / Ingress
families.center_lane_emphasis: Outbound authority work and authenticated callback ingress
families.description: Authority send, poll, callback, and response normalization all remain request-lineage-bound and ingress-receipt-first.
families.note: Authority/provider callbacks enter an authenticated dedupe inbox before any legal-state mutation or settlement update.

families.label: External Delivery / Governance
families.description: Delivery and governance channels append evidence, counters, or attestations without becoming workflow truth.
families.note: Notification and export channels remain observational or attestational unless a durable command or state row already authorized the change.

families.label: Restore / Rebuild
families.description: Restore work rebuilds broker state from outbox, inbox, workflow, and audit truth only.
families.note: This family exists to make queue-loss and restore-law posture explicit rather than implicit runbook prose.

channels.label: Manifest stage dispatch
channels.note: Workers claim stage work from inbox mirrors keyed by manifest and stage, not from broker-local ownership.

channels.label: Upload scan request
channels.note: This channel binds the logical route already frozen in object-storage notifications to one durable inbox/outbox contract.

channels.label: Upload validation recheck
channels.note: Metadata changes may trigger rechecks, but broker redelivery cannot widen upload legality or customer-visible success posture.

channels.label: Evidence manifest binding
channels.note: Binding work must remain idempotent across repeated storage events and queue redelivery.

channels.label: Preview registration
channels.note: The derived preview can be rebuilt from retained evidence and durable projection rules if the broker is lost.

channels.label: Authority transmit dispatch
channels.note: Any redrive must recheck token lineage, client binding, and send legality immediately before transmission.

channels.label: Authority reconciliation poll
channels.note: Recovery resumes from persisted reconciliation control contracts, not from transport logs or consumer-local retry counters.

channels.label: Authority callback ingress
channels.note: This row intentionally has no outbox. The external provider edge authenticates and checkpoints into the inbox boundary first.

channels.label: Authority payload normalization
channels.note: This row bridges immutable payload storage to the same dedupe inbox used for direct callbacks and recovery payloads.

channels.label: Transactional notification send
channels.note: The broker may fan out transactional notifications, but the durable delivery binding still governs audience, masking, and retry posture.

channels.label: Masked export delivery attestation
channels.note: Masked export and restricted export delivery remain distinct channels even when they derive from the same manifest lineage.

channels.label: Restricted export delivery attestation
channels.note: This row intentionally stays separate from the masked-export lane to prevent silent disclosure drift.

channels.label: Retention limitation audit
channels.note: At-least-once object lifecycle events require durable dedupe against retention event identity.

channels.label: Restore archive index
channels.note: This channel makes queue-rebuild law visible: restore indexing may be replayed safely from durable checkpoint evidence.

orderingPolicies.label: Per-manifest ordered partition
orderingPolicies.note: This keeps one manifest from becoming a distributed race between worker instances.

orderingPolicies.label: Per-upload-session ordered partition
orderingPolicies.note: This matches the upload recovery law that a session keeps one governed identity across reconnect and retry.

orderingPolicies.label: Per-authority-interaction ordered partition
orderingPolicies.note: This row keeps request-backed authority recovery deterministic after queue or worker loss.

orderingPolicies.label: Authority delivery dedupe partition
orderingPolicies.note: This is the key transport boundary that keeps provider callback duplication from becoming legal-state duplication.

orderingPolicies.label: Per-delivery-binding partition
orderingPolicies.note: The binding hash is a safer ordering dimension than recipient address or export filename alone.

orderingPolicies.label: Per-checkpoint restore partition
orderingPolicies.note: This row makes disaster-recovery admissibility mechanically explainable.

retryPolicies.label: Worker dispatch backoff
retryPolicies.note: A blocked or poisoned worker dispatch must not be hidden behind endless requeue loops.

retryPolicies.label: Artifact processing backoff
retryPolicies.note: At-least-once storage events and downstream retries must converge onto one immutable object-version identity.

retryPolicies.label: Authority send and poll backoff
retryPolicies.note: No blind resend is legal just because a message left the broker without confirmation of downstream completion.

retryPolicies.label: Authority ingress normalization backoff
retryPolicies.note: Callback duplicates are expected and lawful only when they collapse onto one canonical ingress receipt.

retryPolicies.label: Delivery evidence backoff
retryPolicies.note: Delivery and governance channels stay observational; repeated transport failure must not mutate workflow truth by implication.

retryPolicies.label: Restore and rebuild backoff
retryPolicies.note: Restore-time queue work is admissibility-bound and therefore intentionally conservative.

dedupePolicies.label: Command receipt + manifest dedupe
dedupePolicies.note: This policy prevents a duplicate broker delivery from inventing a second stage runner.

dedupePolicies.label: Object version + event dedupe
dedupePolicies.note: This policy matches the storage event route contract from pc_0051.

dedupePolicies.label: Authority request identity dedupe
dedupePolicies.note: This policy is the operational form of the no-blind-resend rule.

dedupePolicies.label: Authority delivery dedupe
dedupePolicies.note: This policy is the critical callback-ingress safety barrier for the whole messaging topology.

dedupePolicies.label: Delivery binding dedupe
dedupePolicies.note: This policy prevents notification or export evidence from double-counting on repeated callback delivery.

dedupePolicies.label: Restore checkpoint identity dedupe
dedupePolicies.note: This keeps restore queue rebuild admissibility-bound instead of best-effort.

resumeIsolationAtlas.topologyModeLabel: Disposable shared cache + route-bound resume envelopes + strict partition isolation
resumeIsolationAtlas.summary: The shared cache accelerates server-authored projections and hashed resume envelopes, but every reuse path stays subordinate to route, session, access, masking, and object identity. Cache loss is tolerable; cache broadening is not.
notes: Caches and resume stores are rebuildable accelerators, never the legal source of route or visibility truth.
Raw resume tokens remain transport material only. Persisted state stores hashed envelopes, frontier, and invalidation posture.
Tenant, client, access, masking, route, object, and preview drift all beat TTL immediately.

families.label: Manifest experience
families.description: Manifest continuity binds shell stability, route identity, and same-object posture before any cached frame may render as current.

families.label: Collaboration workspace
families.description: Workspace snapshots require visibility partition identity and read-only downgrade whenever access or masking posture drifts.

families.label: Client portal workspace
families.description: Customer-safe portal continuity is route- and masking-bound and may never replay broader staff visibility into a narrower session.

families.label: Upload-session recovery
families.description: Upload-session restore is request-binding- and rebase-bound; a stale cached lane may restore view context only, never mutation authority.

families.label: Native hydration
families.description: Native hydration binds session lineage, projection guard, schema compatibility, and optional preview subject before first paint or scene restore.

partitionRows.label: Manifest frame partition
partitionRows.note: Manifest speed paths remain same-object and same-shell only; no object-id-only shortcut is lawful.

partitionRows.label: Workspace snapshot partition
partitionRows.note: Workspace caching carries visibility partition identity so staff workspace deltas never leak across support, customer-safe, or narrower access slices.

partitionRows.label: Portal workspace partition
partitionRows.note: Portal caches remain customer-safe and route-bound; they cannot be widened into staff-visible or broader request context.

partitionRows.label: Upload-session recovery partition
partitionRows.note: Upload-session restore binds to request rebase lineage and schema compatibility rather than optimistic browser memory alone.

partitionRows.label: Native hydration partition
partitionRows.note: Native restore must carry the exact legality envelope, including session lineage and projection guard, before cached state may render.

resumeRows.label: Manifest experience resume binding
resumeRows.note: Raw tokens remain transport-only. Cached manifest resume state stores hashes, frontier, and legality posture only.

resumeRows.label: Workspace resume binding
resumeRows.note: Workspace resume stays bound to visibility partition and read-only downgrade posture; replay cannot outrun narrower access.

resumeRows.label: Portal workspace resume binding
resumeRows.note: Portal resume law stays customer-safe and session-bound; a prior token cannot widen visibility after invite upgrade, masking drift, or session reissue.

resumeRows.label: Upload-session recovery binding
resumeRows.note: Upload-session recovery may restore view context only. Mutation and attachment remain blocked until live request legality is re-established.

resumeRows.label: Native hydration cursor binding
resumeRows.note: Native hydration persists hashes and legality guards, never raw resume tokens, and must purge local derivatives together on drift.

invalidationRows.label: Tenant switch
invalidationRows.note: Cross-tenant reuse is never lawful, even when the same principal identity appears in both tenants.

invalidationRows.label: Client switch
invalidationRows.note: Client scope narrowing must not leave broader cached variants mounted behind the same tenant shell.

invalidationRows.label: Privilege downgrade
invalidationRows.note: Access narrowing must clear broader variants immediately rather than waiting for TTL expiry.

invalidationRows.label: Masking tightening
invalidationRows.note: Masking drift is a first-class invalidation event because weaker cached views can leak broader evidence or customer detail.

invalidationRows.label: Route or object drift
invalidationRows.note: Same-shell continuity requires same-object continuity; a new route or object cannot inherit a stale cache envelope.

invalidationRows.label: Session revoked
invalidationRows.note: Revocation clears structured cache, resume metadata, NSUserActivity, previews, temp exports, and local indices together.

invalidationRows.label: Step-up completed
invalidationRows.note: Step-up completion changes the effective session challenge state, so cached command or resume lineage from before the challenge cannot be replayed.

invalidationRows.label: Request rebase
invalidationRows.note: Upload-session recovery must fail closed when request binding or base generation changes underneath the cached shell.

invalidationRows.label: Schema incompatible
invalidationRows.note: Schema incompatibility triggers purge or rebase rather than optimistic render because stale contracts can imply false legality.

invalidationRows.label: Cache-only restoration
invalidationRows.note: Cache-only restore is lawful only as bounded visual continuity. It never reopens a mutation-capable posture by itself.

localSharedRows.label: Shared projection snapshot
localSharedRows.note: Projection snapshots are the main shared-cache acceleration target, but still require exact security-context keys.

localSharedRows.label: Hashed resume envelope
localSharedRows.note: Resume metadata may persist as hashes, frontier, and legality posture only. Raw tokens never qualify.

localSharedRows.label: Upload-session rebase envelope
localSharedRows.note: Upload progress may restore within one request-binding window only; broader persistence is forbidden.

localSharedRows.label: Native hydration snapshot
localSharedRows.note: Native disk cache exists only for lawful relaunch and restoration, never as an offline authority for mutation.

localSharedRows.label: Browser restore hint
localSharedRows.note: Browser restore hints stay ephemeral and session-bound so refresh continuity does not become sticky local history.

localSharedRows.label: Visibility invalidation tombstone
localSharedRows.note: Shared invalidation tombstones help fail closed after access or masking drift without preserving stale content locally.

localSharedRows.label: Temporary preview or export artifact
localSharedRows.note: Temporary previews and exports are local derivatives only and must purge on route, masking, or preview-subject drift.

localSharedRows.label: Raw resume token
localSharedRows.note: A raw resume token is transport material only and may never become a cache key, local blob, or shared record.

localSharedRows.label: Session cookie or bearer credential
localSharedRows.note: Interactive session material belongs in browser cookie handling or Keychain-class storage, not in the cache or resume store.

localSharedRows.label: Authority or provider credential
localSharedRows.note: Authority credentials, DSNs, provider secrets, and similar material remain outside the cache boundary entirely.

contractRows.label: Manifest experience continuity
contractRows.note: Manifest continuity is governed by stream recovery plus cache isolation; the cache never invents freshness or current route legality.

contractRows.label: Workspace continuity
contractRows.note: Workspace continuity requires exact visibility partition identity and stream catch-up before live delivery is current again.

contractRows.label: Client portal continuity
contractRows.note: Portal continuity stays customer-safe and exact-context only; broader staff state may not bleed through cache reuse.

contractRows.label: Upload-session recovery
contractRows.note: Upload continuity is request-binding- and rebase-driven rather than a free-form stream token cache.

contractRows.label: Native hydration continuity
contractRows.note: Native restore requires compatibility-before-render and same-object same-shell legality even when local disk cache exists.

telemetrySignalAtlas.summary: One environment-scoped OTLP gateway receives direct SDK and workload-agent traffic, applies redaction, sampling, routing, and backpressure policy, and then sends each signal family to its first-party backend or a strictly allowlisted secondary overlay.
notes: The atlas emphasizes signal law, correlation, and export boundaries rather than vendor console widgets.
Exporter failure degrades telemetry only; runtime correctness and mandatory audit capture continue independently.
Security, privacy, and audit-link lanes remain first-party only.

families.label: Traces
families.summary: Authority, filing, amendment, retention, erasure, replay, and gate-block spans are always kept.
families.sampling_summary: Tail sample 10% generic traffic while always keeping error and compliance-critical traces
families.scrub_summary: Gateway allowlist, redaction, and hash transforms apply before export.

emission_rows.label: Runtime emitters
emission_rows.detail: Northbound API and session gateway · Manifest orchestrator · Authority gateway
inspector_lines: Northbound API and session gateway: Audit gate, session, and approval events remain durable even when ordinary request traces are not kept.
Manifest orchestrator: Manifest allocation, continuation, replay, recovery, and gate-block facts still append to audit.
Authority gateway: Authority transmit and reconcile audit evidence, request hashes, and submission lineage remain durable even if a generic trace is not retained.

emission_rows.label: Resource identity
emission_rows.detail: service.name · service.namespace · deployment.environment.name · service.instance.id · taxat.release.candidate_hash · taxat.build.artifact_digest · taxat.schema.bundle_hash · taxat.workspace.id
inspector_lines: Required resource attributes: service.name, service.namespace, deployment.environment.name, service.instance.id, taxat.release.candidate_hash, taxat.build.artifact_digest, taxat.schema.bundle_hash, taxat.workspace.id
Optional resource attributes: taxat.manifest.lineage_trace_ref, taxat.authority.binding_ref, taxat.manual.checkpoint_ref, telemetry.distro.name, telemetry.distro.version

collector_rows.label: Environment gateway collectors
collector_rows.detail: Bounded sending queues, retry with backoff, persistent WAL for remote exporters, and failure-aware degraded mode that protects runtime correctness.
inspector_lines: Deployment scope: One environment-scoped gateway tier per stable runtime namespace and one isolated gateway for ephemeral preview.
Processors: memory_limiter, batch, filter/high_cardinality_drop, redaction/allowlist, transform/hash_selected_fields, tail_sampling, routing/by_signal_family
Ingress mode: Single environment OTLP ingress for direct SDKs and workload agents

collector_rows.label: Priority traces
collector_rows.detail: Bounded queue, gzip compression, and WAL-backed exporter queue on gateway nodes.
inspector_lines: Processors: memory_limiter, batch, tail_sampling, attributes/correlation_enrichment, filter/vendor_export_allowlist
Export targets: backend.first_party_traces, backend.vendor_monitoring_overlay
Critical traces stay sampled-in even when generic traffic is reduced.

backend_rows.label: First-party trace backend
backend_rows.detail: restricted runtime and incident-response operators only
inspector_lines: Ownership boundary: FIRST_PARTY_REQUIRED
Storage class: hot query window plus short warm retention
Binding state: BLOCKED_BY_PLATFORM_PROVIDER_SELECTION
Primary trace store remains unresolved until the platform provider is chosen.

backend_rows.label: Sentry-compatible monitoring overlay
inspector_lines: Ownership boundary: VENDOR_SECONDARY_OVERLAY
Storage class: secondary issue and sampled-trace view
Imported from pc_0044 and treated as a secondary sink for allowlisted traces, exceptions, and release markers.

correlation_rows.label: Mandatory keys
correlation_rows.detail: trace_id · span_id · tenant_id · client_id · manifest_id · root_manifest_id · workflow_item_id · authority_operation_id · submission_record_id · request_hash · access_binding_hash · code_build_id
inspector_lines: Mandatory keys: trace_id, span_id, tenant_id, client_id, manifest_id, root_manifest_id, workflow_item_id, authority_operation_id, submission_record_id, request_hash, access_binding_hash, code_build_id
Audit join anchors: manifest_id, submission_record_id, authority_operation_id, nightly_batch_run_ref + nightly_window_key
Fallback join keys: error_id, manifest_lineage_trace_ref, workflow_item_id

correlation_rows.label: Lineage strip
correlation_rows.detail: Runtime span -> environment gateway -> first-party trace backend -> audit join via trace_id and manifest anchors
inspector_lines: Runtime span -> environment gateway -> first-party trace backend -> audit join via trace_id and manifest anchors
Telemetry remains queryable without replacing append-only audit evidence.

families.lineage_strip: Runtime span -> environment gateway -> first-party trace backend -> audit join via trace_id and manifest anchors

source_refs.rationale: Operational, security, and privacy telemetry must remain distinct from append-only audit evidence.
Every signal family needs correlation keys that bind telemetry back to manifest, authority, workflow, and release lineage.
Compliance-capable traces must preserve manifest-rooted structure and explicit sampling posture for critical flows.
Reliability, security, privacy, and delivery metrics are mandatory even when backend product choice remains open.
Structured logging must include correlation keys and message-template posture while excluding raw secrets and sensitive bodies.
Sampling and retention decisions for telemetry may never erase required audit history or collapse restricted event families into generic logs.
Critical spans, authority mutations, and blocking gates require explicit trace and audit linkage rather than best-effort telemetry.
Visibility classes, masking posture, and retention state have to survive across the telemetry and audit split.
Telemetry secrets, headers, and downstream credentials must stay in governed secret boundaries and out of logs or repo-tracked payloads.
The runtime topology already separates operator access, orchestrator, workers, authority gateway, broker, control store, and audit store, so telemetry topology must match those boundaries.
Release correlation requires build digest, candidate identity, schema bundle, and admissibility lineage inside telemetry resources and joins.
The Sentry-compatible monitoring workspace already exists as a secondary vendor overlay and must not be mistaken for the primary telemetry substrate.
The dependency register keeps core OpenTelemetry backends in procurement or platform-choice posture, so this card must stay portable and fail closed.

inspector_notes: Authority, filing, amendment, retention, erasure, replay, and gate-block spans are always kept.
No compliance-significant span may rely on best-effort probabilistic sampling alone.
Authority traces also carry access-binding and authority-binding refs.

families.label: Metrics
families.scrub_summary: Gateway allowlist, redaction, and hash transforms apply before export.

emission_rows.label: Runtime emitters
emission_rows.detail: Stage workers · Read-side projector and stream broker
inspector_lines: Stage workers: Worker backlog, retry, and failure facts still persist through audit and workflow records.
Read-side projector and stream broker: Projection rebuildability is proven from durable truth, not from continuous trace capture.

collector_rows.label: Metrics

backend_rows.label: First-party metric backend

Native operator workspace: Scene-restore, cache-rebase, and detach-window facts remain durable via workflow and audit rather than local-only telemetry.

source_refs.rationale: Operational, security, and privacy telemetry must remain distinct from append-only audit evidence.
Every signal family needs correlation keys that bind telemetry back to manifest, authority, workflow, and release lineage.
Compliance-capable traces must preserve manifest-rooted structure and explicit sampling posture for critical flows.
Reliability, security, privacy, and delivery metrics are mandatory even when backend product choice remains open.
Structured logging must include correlation keys and message-template posture while excluding raw secrets and sensitive bodies.
Sampling and retention decisions for telemetry may never erase required audit history or collapse restricted event families into generic logs.
Critical spans, authority mutations, and blocking gates require explicit trace and audit linkage rather than best-effort telemetry.
Visibility classes, masking posture, and retention state have to survive across the telemetry and audit split.
Telemetry secrets, headers, and downstream credentials must stay in governed secret boundaries and out of logs or repo-tracked payloads.
The runtime topology already separates operator access, orchestrator, workers, authority gateway, broker, control store, and audit store, so telemetry topology must match those boundaries.
Release correlation requires build digest, candidate identity, schema bundle, and admissibility lineage inside telemetry resources and joins.
The Sentry-compatible monitoring workspace already exists as a secondary vendor overlay and must not be mistaken for the primary telemetry substrate.
The dependency register keeps core OpenTelemetry backends in procurement or platform-choice posture, so this card must stay portable and fail closed.

inspector_notes: Security telemetry is separately restricted from general runtime logs and metrics.
Restricted security telemetry never leaves the first-party boundary.
